rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical trans-chloroformyloxylation of unactivated cyclic and acyclic alkenes using a diaryl diselenide catalyst, Bu4NCl as chloride source, and DMF as both solvent and formyloxy source
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of the diaryl diselenide (or PhSeCl) catalyst generates an electrophilic PhSe+ species that activates the alkene; the chloride/DMF then close the cycle to deliver trans-1,2-functionalization

key_intermediates.name: electrophilic phenylselenenyl cation (PhSe+) generated by anodic oxidation
seleniranium ion (bridged episelenonium from alkene + PhSe+)
beta-chloro alkyl phenyl selenide after chloride opening of seleniranium
trans-1,2-chloro formate ester product (DMF-derived formyloxy + Cl across alkene)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the diaryl diselenide/PhSeCl catalyst to a PhSe+ electrophile, which adds to the alkene to form a seleniranium ion; trans-opening by chloride (or by DMF followed by hydrolysis to the formate) installs Cl and the formyloxy group on opposite faces, regenerating the selenium catalyst.

probe_reagents.name: water additive (5–50 equiv) screening
probe_reagents.observation: in presence of water chlorohydrins are formed as side products; anhydrous DMF is required to obtain the trans-chloroformyloxylation product (DMF acts as O-nucleophile that is then hydrolyzed to the formate)

probe_reagents.name: diaryl diselenide electronic/steric variants (3a–d)
probe_reagents.observation: screened to probe steric and electronic effects on the selenium catalyst; PhSeCl proved superior, requiring 50 mol% loading

notes: Mechanism is proposed by analogy to Denmark's diselenide-catalyzed cis-dichlorination (which invokes a PhSe+ seleniranium with double SN2 by Cl-). Here the electrochemical variant replaces the chemical oxidant with the anode; trans-selectivity arises from single nucleophilic ring-opening by Cl- followed by DMF capture (then hydrolytic workup to formate). With 1,1-disubstituted alkenes vinyl chlorides are obtained, and 1-phenylcyclohex-1-ene gives an allyl chloride, consistent with a cationic seleniranium/carbocation manifold rather than radical addition. No TEMPO/BHT/KIE/CV/DFT studies are reported on these pages.
